rationale: Exceptionally clear and accessible writing that uses effective analogies (mortgage comparison, homeowner analogy) and concrete examples ($50 million water treatment plant, new high school). The numbered breakdown of how the transaction works is particularly well done. Language is genuinely plain without being condescending. Loses a point on structure/flow because the piece is cut off mid-sentence in the 'Why Investors Like Them' section, leaving it incomplete. Brevity is good but the piece has some mild redundancy (the opening scenario and the later explanations cover similar ground). The $50 million example and the library/future-users argument show good specificity. Voice is warm, conversational, and reader-aware throughout. The incompleteness is the main flaw—it undermines the overall effectiveness.
